revision.text: #redirect[[en:Moritachi_no_Kami]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 守達神 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 守達神 | 別名 = 安達神、宇達神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[高志奈男神]]、[[美都多麻比売命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]、[[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]等 }} '''守達神'''（もりたのかみ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 守達神を主祭神として祀る[[長野市]][[七二会]]の[[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]（[[水内郡]]の[[式内社]]守田神社の論社）では建御名方神の第五子とされ、水内開拓の祖神と伝わる。また『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観5年（863年）2月14日条に記される式外社'''宇達神'''と同神と考えられる。また明治初期に書かれた[[守矢氏]]の家系図『神長守矢氏系譜』の中では、守矢氏の二代目とされる[[守宅神]]の別名に'''守田ノ神'''を挙げ、同一視されることもある。 [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]五年二月に[[従五位]]下の「安達神」に従五位上を加授、同七年三月十二日に従五位上「宇達神」に[[従四位]]下を加授したと見え、これらはいずれも守達神とされる[[延川和彦]]「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、15頁。。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子であり、子に[[高志奈男神]]、[[美都多麻比売命]]がいる。 美都多麻比売命（美都玉比売命）は[[片倉辺命]]の子[[児玉彦命]]との間に[[八櫛神]]を生み、この神は守矢氏の五代目とされる「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、34頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） * [[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]（長野県[[長野市]][[七二会]]） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[諏訪氏]] {{デフォルトソート:もりたのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子であり、子に[[高志奈男神]]、[[美都多麻比売命]]がいる。 美都多麻比売命（美都玉比売命）は[[片倉辺命]]の子[[児玉彦命]]との間に[[八櫛神]]を生み、この神は守矢氏の五代目とされる「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、34頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） * [[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]（長野県[[長野市]][[七二会]]） === 宇達神を祀る神社 === * [[宇達神社]]（長野県長野市下宇木） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[諏訪氏]] {{デフォルトソート:もりたのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神もりたのかみ]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 守達神 | 世代名 = | 先代= | 次代= | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 守達神 | 別名 = 安達神、宇達神 | 別称 = | 神階 = [[従四位]]下 | 神格 = 開拓神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[高志奈男神]]、[[美都多麻比売命]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]、[[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]等 | 関連氏族 = [[守矢氏]] }} '''守達神'''（もりたのかみ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 [[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|諏訪大社前宮若御子社]] == 概要 == 守達神を主祭神として祀る[[長野市]][[七二会]]の[[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]（[[水内郡]]の[[式内社]]守田神社の論社）では建御名方神の第五子とされ、水内開拓の祖神と伝わる。また『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観5年（863年）2月14日条に記される式外社'''宇達神'''と同神と考えられる。ただし宇達神を祀る長野市下宇木の宇達神社では、宇達神（宇逵神）は'''泥土立神'''の転訛と伝える。また明治初期に書かれた[[守矢氏]]の家系図『神長守矢氏系譜』の中では、守矢氏の二代目とされる[[守宅神]]の別名に'''守田ノ神'''を挙げ、同一視されることもある。 [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]五年二月に[[従五位]]下の「安達神」に従五位上を加授、同七年三月十二日に従五位上「宇達神」に[[従四位]]下を加授したと見え、これらはいずれも守達神とされる[[延川和彦]]「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、15頁。。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] [[建御名方神]]と[[八坂刀売神]]の子であり、子に[[高志奈男神]]、[[美都多麻比売命]]がいる。 美都多麻比売命（美都玉比売命）は[[片倉辺命]]の子[[児玉彦命]]との間に[[八櫛神]]を生み、この神は守矢氏の五代目とされる「神長守矢氏系譜」『諏訪史料叢書.巻28』諏訪教育会、昭和11年、34頁。。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） * [[守田神社 (七二会)|守田神社]]（長野県[[長野市]][[七二会]]） === 宇達神を祀る神社 === * [[宇達神社]]（長野県長野市下宇木） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[諏訪大社]] * [[諏訪氏]] {{デフォルトソート:もりたのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神もりたのかみ]]

This thing related to the Suwa Faith does in fact exist so an article translating the Japanese wikipedia is worthwhile at some point __NOINDEX__
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:守達神]] [[en:Moritatsu-no-Kami]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏}}

'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.

According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya Clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}

'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.

'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya Clan|ja|守矢氏}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.

Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.

'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.

== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]

== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.

{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売神}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神}} with {{ill|Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65260779]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]] | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65260779]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]] | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|守達神|version=76088823|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]]
[[Category:Suwa faith]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65260779]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]] | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65260779]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]] | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)

His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:守達神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65260779]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]] | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.

== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome]]. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.

'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. == Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
