{{Infobox deity | name = Morita-no-Kami | type = Japanese | other_names = {{nihongo|Adachi-no-Kami|安達神}}, {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}} | deified = Pioneer deity | parents = {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome|1=Yasakatome}} | siblings = | children = {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | associated_with = {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}, {{nihongo|Morita Shrine|守田神社}} and others | text = Japanese mythology }}
'''Morita-no-Kami''' ({{nihongo|守達神|もりたのかみ}}) is a deity in the local tradition of {{nihongo|Suwa Faith|諏訪信仰}}, centered in {{nihongo|Nagano Prefecture|長野県}}. The deity is worshiped primarily as a pioneer god.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Young Deity Shrine (Wakamiko-sha) at Suwa Taisha]]
== Overview == At {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City, the primary shrine dedicated to Morita-no-Kami, the deity is considered the fifth son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and is celebrated as the ancestral god of development in the region. Morita-no-Kami is often equated with the deity {{nihongo|Udachi-no-Kami|宇達神}}, mentioned in the {{nihongo|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|日本三代実録}} in 863 CE, and worshiped at {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Nagano City. According to tradition, Udachi-no-Kami is sometimes identified as a form of {{nihongo|Deidatsurajin|泥土立神}}. Additionally, a genealogy compiled by the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}} in the early Meiji period equates Morita-no-Kami with the second-generation family head {{nihongo|Moritaku-no-Kami|守宅神|1=Moritaku-no-Kami}}.
In 863 CE, "Adachi-no-Kami" was promoted to {{nihongo|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and in 865 CE, "Udachi-no-Kami" was elevated to {{nihongo|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}. These honors likely refer to Morita-no-Kami.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumb|Genealogy of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Morita-no-Kami is the son of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome|1=Yasakatome}}. His children include {{ill|Koshinao-no-Kami|ja|高志奈男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshinao-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|美都多麻比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mitutama-hime-no-Mikoto bore {{ill|Yakushi-no-Kami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} with {{ill|Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe no Mikoto|lt=Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|ja|片倉辺命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakurabe-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Yakushi-no-Kami is considered the fifth head of the Moriya clan.
== Shrines Dedicated to Morita-no-Kami == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Kamisha Maemiya, {{nihongo|Wakamiko Shrine|若御子社}} ({{nihongo|Chino City|茅野市}}, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Toyohira|豊平}}, Chino City, Nagano) * {{nihongo|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya)|十五社神社}} ({{nihongo|Okaya City|岡谷市}}, Nagano) * {{ill|Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja|守田神社 (七二会)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{nihongo|Nanaiai|七二会}}, Nagano City, Nagano)
=== Shrines for Udachi-no-Kami === * {{ill|Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja|宇達神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Udachi Shrine (Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano City, Nagano)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}
